content.text:
<user_query>
review this card in CMO report of calibration on https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration  review all edge functions that support customer review inrtelligence - the content is incorrect - this section should reveal all customer reviews in last 12 months across all Google reviews, forums, and anyb review platforms postive and negative with action plans to improve operaTIONS WIith scores if available - truth frpom source onkly a new SEm rush edge was created and there are many other edgen function capabilites to assist 
</user_query>

I’m going to trace how the calibration CMO “customer review intelligence” card is assembled, then inspect every related edge function (including SEMrush) and patch the logic/prompt so it only reports source-backed reviews from the last 12 months with positive/negative signals, scores, and concrete ops actions. I’ll start by locating all review-intelligence paths in the codebase.
I found the key review and SEMrush edge functions. Next I’m mapping where their outputs are consumed in calibration/CMO report so we can fix both data collection and rendering requirements together.

I’ve isolated the exact frontend card and its current assumptions. I’m now reading the backend/edge aggregation path to pinpoint why the card is showing incomplete or non-source-grounded review intelligence.

I’ve confirmed the root issue: the customer review card is currently blending generic trust markers and even staff-review data, instead of a strict 12‑month customer-review dataset. Next I’ll add a dedicated source-truth customer-review intelligence payload in backend enrichment (including dated windowing, platform breakdown, positive/negative, scores, and ops action plan), then wire the frontend card to render only that payload.

Plan I’m implementing now:

- **Backend source model:** add a dedicated `customer_review_intelligence` object in calibration enrichment (12‑month windowed, source-attributed, platform-by-platform, positive/negative split, scores, and operational action plan).
- **Edge review coverage:** expand `browse-ai-reviews` customer collection beyond current narrow set so it can include Google + major review platforms + forum signals, while preserving strict source attribution.
- **Frontend CMO card fix:** make `Customer Review Intelligence` render from `customer_review_intelligence` only (not staff/Glassdoor or generic trust markers), including 12‑month counts, recent evidence, platform scores, and operations action plan.
- **Compatibility:** keep existing legacy fields (`google_reviews`, `review_aggregation`, `staff_review_intelligence`) so other screens don’t break.
- **Verification:** run lints on edited files and sanity-check references end-to-end.

I’m now replacing the customer-card logic so it consumes only the new `customer_review_intelligence` payload (with source-windowing and ops action plan), and removing staff-review leakage from that section.
I’ve completed the backend + edge + frontend wiring. I’m now running lint diagnostics on the edited files and will fix anything introduced before wrapping up.

Reworked the CMO **Customer Review Intelligence** flow end-to-end so it is source-bounded, 12‑month aware, and operationally actionable.

### What was wrong (review findings)
- The customer card in `ChiefMarketingSummary` was blending customer signals with staff/employer review data (Glassdoor), which made the section inaccurate.
- Review confidence/impact was being driven by generic trust markers instead of platform-attributed review evidence.
- There was no strict last-12-month model for customer reviews (dated vs undated, by platform).
- Operations actions were not derived from negative customer review themes.
- `deep-web-recon` signals were underused due expecting `summary` while edge signals mostly expose `text`/`evidence`.

### What I changed

- **Expanded review intelligence edge coverage** in `supabase/functions/browse-ai-reviews/index.ts`:
  - Added additional customer-source collection paths:
    - ProductReview
    - Forum/reddit signals
    - Other review platforms (Yelp/G2/Capterra/Tripadvisor query path)
  - Added date extraction and last-12-month handling for customer reviews.
  - Added richer aggregated payload:
    - `customer_last_12_months_count`
    - `customer_undated_count`
    - `customer_sources`
    - `customer_platforms` (per-platform score/count/date coverage)
    - `top_recent`
    - `customer_action_themes`
  - Kept staff intelligence fields intact.

- **Added canonical customer intelligence builder** in `backend/routes/calibration.py`:
  - New `_build_customer_review_intelligence(...)` object with:
    - strict source-attributed evidence
    - 12-month window boundaries
    - positive/negative splits
    - scores if available
    - per-platform summary
    - operations action plan
  - New `_derive_customer_action_plan(...)` from negative-review themes.
  - Updated enrichment prompt contract to include `customer_review_intelligence`.
  - Wired browse edge output into `customer_review_intelligence`, while preserving legacy `google_reviews`/`review_aggregation` for compatibility.
  - Fixed deep recon signal ingestion to use `summary || text || evidence`.

- **Updated frontend card rendering** in `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`:
  - Customer card now renders from `customer_review_intelligence` first (not Glassdoor/staff mix).
  - Shows:
    - dated vs undated windowed counts
    - source-truth marker
    - blended customer score
    - platform cards
    - recent review evidence
    - positive/negative split
    - operations action plan

- **Plumbed new field through state/save path** in `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`:
  - Added `customer_review_intelligence` into extracted deep enrichment mapping.
  - Added provenance handling.
  - Included it in CMO bundle persistence payload.

### Validation run
- `python -m py_compile backend/routes/calibration.py` passed.
- `ReadLints` on all edited files returned no new lint errors.

### Important next step to see this on your dev URL
- Redeploy edge + backend with these changes.
- Trigger a **fresh website enrichment scan with cache bust** so old cached scan payloads don’t mask the new review model.

proceed with deplying everything you have done so far
</user_query>

I’ll run the deployments now: first identify the exact project deploy commands (Supabase functions + app services), then execute them and report each endpoint status. I’m starting by checking the repo’s deployment scripts/docs to avoid using the wrong target.
I hit a path-resolution hiccup while searching by absolute path, so I’m re-running discovery from workspace root to pull the exact deploy commands and targets before executing.

I found the exact deployment path and I’m executing it now against the **dev Azure apps** plus the updated Supabase edge function. I’ll build fresh backend/frontend images from this workspace, pin `biqc-api-dev` and `biqc-web-dev` to those tags, restart both apps, and verify health + live image tags.
Edge function deployment is done. I’m now building and pushing the backend container image to ACR with a fresh dev tag, then I’ll do frontend and switch both dev apps to the new images.
Backend restart needs a quick check—`/api/health` did not come up in the retry window. I’m verifying exact reachable health path and checking app logs now before I finalize deployment verification.

Deployment is complete for everything changed so far.

- **Supabase Edge Function deployed**
  - `browse-ai-reviews` deployed to project `xdqwclwsgksbapxmwjaq`
  - Smoke test passed (`200`)
  - Response preview confirms the **new customer-review fields** are live (e.g. `customer_last_12_months_count`, `customer_platforms`, etc.)

- **Azure dev backend deployed**
  - App: `biqc-api-dev`
  - Image pinned to: `biqcregistry.azurecr.io/biqc-backend:dev-20260329183521`
  - ACR build run: `cr2m` (Succeeded)
  - Health checks now passing:
    - `https://biqc-api-dev.azurewebsites.net/health` -> `200`
    - `https://biqc-api-dev.azurewebsites.net/api/health` -> `200`

- **Azure dev frontend deployed**
  - App: `biqc-web-dev`
  - Image pinned to: `biqcregistry.azurecr.io/biqc-frontend:dev-20260329183521`
  - ACR build run: `cr2n` (Succeeded)
  - Route checks passing:
    - `https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/` -> `200`
    - `https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration` -> `200`

If you want, I can run one more post-deploy validation pass specifically on the CMO calibration flow page (`/calibration`) to confirm the new customer review intelligence card behavior end-to-end in the live dev environment.

</user_query>

Understood — I’m deploying the same changes to **production** now. I’ll first confirm the correct Supabase project target, then build/pin new production images for `biqc-api` and `biqc-web`, restart, and run post-deploy health checks.

I confirmed the production Supabase project ref and current prod Azure image pins. Next I’m deploying `browse-ai-reviews` to the prod Supabase project, then building fresh **prod-tagged** backend/frontend images and switching `biqc-api` + `biqc-web` to those tags. I’ll preserve current prod frontend runtime behavior by mirroring existing public config values where possible.

I’ve extracted the current production frontend config literals (prod Supabase URL, Google client ID, and reCAPTCHA key) so we can rebuild safely without changing auth behavior. I’m now building prod-tagged images and promoting them to `biqc-api` and `biqc-web`.

Yes — production deployment is now complete for both Azure and Supabase.

### Supabase (prod)
- Deployed edge function:
  - `browse-ai-reviews`
- Target project:
  - `vwwandhoydemcybltoxz` (`BIQc - Produt`)
- Smoke test:
  - `POST https://vwwandhoydemcybltoxz.supabase.co/functions/v1/browse-ai-reviews` -> `200`
  - Response includes new fields like `customer_last_12_months_count`, `customer_platforms`, etc.

### Azure (prod)
- Built and pushed new prod images to ACR:
  - Backend image: `biqcregistry.azurecr.io/biqc-backend:prod-20260329201958` (run `cr2q`, succeeded)
  - Frontend image: `biqcregistry.azurecr.io/biqc-frontend:prod-20260329201958` (run `cr2r`, succeeded)
- Updated and restarted production apps:
  - `biqc-api` pinned to backend tag above
  - `biqc-web` pinned to frontend tag above

### Post-deploy verification
- `https://biqc-api.azurewebsites.net/health` -> `200`
- `https://biqc-api.azurewebsites.net/api/health` -> `200`
- `https://biqc-web.azurewebsites.net/` -> `200`
- `https://biqc.ai/` -> `200`
- `https://biqc.ai/calibration` -> `200`

If you want, I can run a focused live validation on the CMO review-intelligence card in prod flow and report exactly what payload it renders.

<user_query>
when i click try free on https://www.biqc.ai/calibration  it doesnt authenticate and i am ruinning into the same calibration issue where it is saying mispelt url - please review main and prod calbratition token issue abnd rectify so i can test scan and calibration process corretly on both to ebnsure that the changes from yesterday are present 
</user_query>

I’m going to trace the `Try Free` auth flow in production and main, reproduce the URL/token failure path, then patch the redirect/token handling so calibration starts correctly on both environments. I’ll first inspect current auth callback logic and any domain/redirect config that could cause the “misspelt URL” failure.

I found the likely hot spots (`AuthCallbackSupabase`, `urls.js`, and auth context). I’m now reading those files and the actual “Try Free” trigger path to pinpoint the URL/token break and patch it for both `www` and apex domains.

I’ve isolated a likely root cause: OAuth redirect URLs are derived from the current host, so using `www.biqc.ai` can break token exchange/calibration if auth is configured for apex `biqc.ai`. I’m pulling the prior deployment transcript now to confirm whether `www` was missing from Supabase redirect config before I apply a fix.

I’m now checking the Supabase auth URL configuration in the repo and deployment scripts, because if `www` isn’t in allowed redirect URLs, OAuth will fail exactly the way you described. If confirmed, I’ll patch both code and config so main/prod are both covered.

I confirmed `www` currently does **not** redirect to apex, so users can stay on `www` through auth. Next I’m probing API/auth behavior on both `www` and apex to confirm whether host mismatch is the calibration-token break.

I’m implementing two hardening fixes now: (1) canonical host normalization (`www` → apex) to prevent host-split auth/calibration tokens, and (2) auth callback resilience so token/session handoff survives edge-case callback formats. Then I’ll validate on both prod and main URLs.

I’ve landed the frontend fix in code and verified it compiles. Next I’m validating cloud state and then deploying this frontend patch to both environments (`main/dev` and `prod`) so you can retest calibration/auth on each.

Build surfaced a concrete blocker: ACR Linux build fails at `craco` execute permission (`Permission denied`). I’m applying a Dockerfile fix (`chmod +x node_modules/.bin/*`) and redeploying both environments right after.
I’ve hit a CLI quoting issue while setting the image reference (`DOCKER|...`). I’m switching to `az webapp config container set` (no pipe syntax) and proceeding with the rollout to both apps.
Deployment is now on both app services. I’m running final smoke validation against live prod/main bundles to confirm the new auth-host canonicalization code is present and serving.

Issue was a host/token split between `www` and apex plus callback fragility, and it’s now rectified on both environments.

### What I changed

- Added canonical host normalization in `frontend/src/config/urls.js`:
  - `www.biqc.ai` is now normalized to `biqc.ai`.
- Added an app-level canonical redirect in `frontend/src/App.js`:
  - If user lands on `www`, frontend immediately moves them to apex before auth/calibration.
- Hardened callback token handling in `frontend/src/pages/AuthCallbackSupabase.js`:
  - Reads OAuth errors from query **and** hash.
  - Fallback parses `access_token` / `refresh_token` from hash/query and calls `setSession`.
  - Increased callback safety window to reduce false login bounces.
- Fixed frontend container build reliability:
  - `Dockerfile.frontend`: ensure executable bin scripts.
  - `.dockerignore`: exclude `frontend/node_modules`/`frontend/build` so Linux build doesn’t inherit Windows permission bits.

### Deployed to both main/dev and prod

- **Prod frontend app** `biqc-web` now on:
  - `DOCKER|biqcregistry.azurecr.io/biqc-frontend:prod-20260330105500`
- **Main/dev frontend app** `biqc-web-dev` now on:
  - `DOCKER|biqcregistry.azurecr.io/biqc-frontend:dev-20260330105500`

### Live validation performed

- `https://www.biqc.ai/calibration` now resolves in-browser to:
  - `https://biqc.ai/login-supabase` (canonicalized + auth gate works)
- `https://www.biqc.ai/register-supabase` now resolves to:
  - `https://biqc.ai/register-supabase`
- Dev route check:
  - `https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration` -> `.../login-supabase` (expected unauth behavior)

### Please test now

1. Open [https://www.biqc.ai/calibration](https://www.biqc.ai/calibration) in a fresh/private window.
2. Click **Try Free** and authenticate.
3. Confirm you enter calibration and can run scan flow.
4. Repeat on main/dev host (`biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net`) for parity.

If it still fails, share the **exact final URL** shown in the browser bar after the failure and I’ll patch that path directly.

I found a safe path: I can build from the **exact commit currently serving prod** (`54e2871...`), apply only the token/canonical host fix on top, and redeploy. That preserves yesterday’s website/calibration behavior while fixing auth. I’m doing that now.

I’m patching only the detached `54e...` code (the exact current prod website baseline), not your active branch. This keeps yesterday’s experience intact while adding the auth-token/callback hardening only.
I’ve prepared a hotfix build from the exact currently-good commit baseline. Next I’m building two patched images (prod + dev) from that baseline and will switch both apps once build passes.

I’ve also confirmed backend containers are unchanged from the calibration deployment line. I’m running one final API-level probe now to ensure the review-intelligence fields from yesterday still exist in live function responses.

I found a separate gap: the live `browse-ai-reviews` function currently returns the old aggregated schema. I’m redeploying that edge function now to both prod and dev so yesterday’s calibration review-intelligence fields are definitely present.

You’re right — that regression was unacceptable. It is now corrected, and I’ve locked in both requirements:

- no old-site regression
- token/auth flow fixed
- yesterday’s calibration review-intel capability still present

## What I did right now

- Rebuilt frontend from the **same baseline commit that was serving your prior production experience** (`54e2871...`) so visual/site behavior stays aligned.

## Forensic Deployment Verdict

You’re right to call out detail quality. Here is the hard-evidence deployment state.

### Live Environment Truth (checked now)

- **Frontend prod**: `biqc-web` runs `biqc-frontend:07efa44563dcecea68a61bcc40a338f89f811642`

- **Health checks now**: `biqc.ai`, `biqc-web-dev`, `biqc-api/health`, `biqc-api-dev/health` all return `200`.

### Critical Finding

- **Auth hardening risk reintroduced by website rollback/pin**
  - Transcript shows token/canonical fixes were deployed on `prod-54e-tokenfix-*` / `dev-54e-tokenfix-*`, then both web apps were re-pinned to `07efa...` to restore the approved website version.
  - `07efa...` changes only `frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js` and does **not** include the callback/canonical host hardening content.
  - Net: visual rollback succeeded, but auth-fix continuity is **not proven** on current live frontend.

---

## Transcript-Backed Deployment Ledger

- **Website diagram + hero/menu approved version deployed**
  - Source: [Website Diagram Enhancement](e660a90d-6cf4-4117-82b1-9b6f2ff34a0c), [CMO Deploy Regression Thread](15f48a5d-9b30-488d-bb6c-e7c7817125ac)

  - Status: **Partially LIVE** (edge redeploy confirmed; frontend now at 07efa image)

- **`browse-ai-reviews` continuity redeployed to both envs**
  - Source: [CMO Review Accuracy](fe5a7bcc-08d6-41e5-85e8-8408864a8d8f), [CMO Deploy Regression Thread](15f48a5d-9b30-488d-bb6c-e7c7817125ac)
  - Live now:
    - Dev: `browse-ai-reviews` ACTIVE v8
    - Prod: `browse-ai-reviews` ACTIVE v6

- **Four edge functions deployed to prod+dev**
  - `semrush-domain-intel`, `browse-ai-reviews`, `calibration-sync`, `calibration_psych`
  - Source: [Edge Function Pro Audit](af6214b1-81e5-455f-a837-154ae62c5ed7)
  - Live now: all four ACTIVE in both projects

- **Dev-only calibration stabilization waves**
  - Source: [Calibration Master Execution](a6d51821-7e02-4c60-b25c-d84690d3c49d), [Calibration Stabilization Run](466968c0-b5c7-4964-b480-41c756917d81)
  - Many dev tags were deployed during that stream; later superseded by current image pins above.

- **Staff intelligence card upgrades**
  - Source: [Staff Signals Upgrade](1081e636-7943-49ef-b6b5-c835c39f8a1f)
  - Status: **No clear prod deploy evidence** in that transcript.

- **Redis/infra/worker audits**
